text.title: Minimum Product Frameworks Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Minimum Product Frameworks. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Frame chosen from goal; not from acronym preference.
rule.rationale: Frame chosen from goal; not from acronym preference.

rule.statement: Use exactly four: MVP / MLP / MMP / MMR. Don't invent new ones.
rule.rationale: Use exactly four: MVP / MLP / MMP / MMR. Don't invent new ones.

rule.statement: Frame routes to a specific planning methodology.
rule.rationale: Frame routes to a specific planning methodology.

rule.statement: Frame reassessed each quarter as goals shift.
rule.rationale: Frame reassessed each quarter as goals shift.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-minimum-product-frameworks.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
